description:
You are a build-/release-engineer who has just received a raw dump of all the Java artifacts that were published during last night’s CI/CD pipeline run.  
The dump has already been copied for you to the path  

/home/user/project/artifacts/list.txt  

Your job is to:  

1. Parse that file in the terminal (using any CLI utilities you like, e.g. sort and uniq).  
2. Produce a frequency report that shows, for every distinct artifact name, how many times it appears in the dump.  
3. Make sure the report is written to the exact path  

/home/user/project/reports/artifact_frequency.log  

4. If the directory /home/user/project/reports/ does not yet exist, create it first.  
5. Ownership and permissions must remain as the current unprivileged user; directory mode should be at least 755 so the automated grader can read the result.  
6. Output format requirements (***these will be checked verbatim by an automated test***):  
   • One artifact per line.  
   • Each line consists of two columns separated by a single space:  
     – The first column is the decimal frequency count (no leading zeros).  
     – The second column is the full artifact filename exactly as it appears in list.txt.  
   • Lines must be ordered primarily by descending frequency; for artifacts with the same frequency, order them alphabetically (ASCII) by their full name.  
   • No header line, no trailing blank lines, and each line must end with a single newline character (Unix LF).  
   • Example of the required layout (the numbers shown here are **only examples**—your actual counts must come from list.txt):  
     17 artifact-example-0.0.1.jar  
     3  artifact-zzz-9.9.9.jar  

7. When you are done, verify the contents of /home/user/project/reports/artifact_frequency.log in the terminal so that the grader will capture your console output.

Hints (you are not forced to use them):  
• A classic pipeline is “sort | uniq -c | sort -k1,1nr -k2,2” followed by column clean-up.  
• Remember that uniq only collapses adjacent identical lines, so pre-sorting is crucial.  
• Pay close attention to the single-space delimiter; tools like awk or sed may help to re-format.
truth:
Initial on-disk state before the agent starts:  

1. Directory structure  
   /home/user/project/                 (0700, owned by user)  
   /home/user/project/artifacts/       (0700, owned by user)  
2. Seed data file  
   Path: /home/user/project/artifacts/list.txt  
   Mode: 0644  
   Content (13 lines, each ending with LF):  
     artifact-alpha-1.0.0.jar  
     artifact-beta-2.1.0.jar  
     artifact-alpha-1.0.0.jar  
     artifact-gamma-3.3.7.jar  
     artifact-alpha-1.0.0.jar  
     artifact-beta-2.1.0.jar  
     artifact-delta-4.0.0.jar  
     artifact-epsilon-5.2.1.jar  
     artifact-gamma-3.3.7.jar  
     artifact-gamma-3.3.7.jar  
     artifact-beta-2.1.0.jar  
     artifact-delta-4.0.0.jar  
     artifact-beta-2.1.0.jar  

Expected artefact after successful task completion:  

1. Newly created directory  
   /home/user/project/reports/         (at least 0755, owned by user)  

2. Frequency report  
   Path: /home/user/project/reports/artifact_frequency.log  
   Mode: anything ≥ 0644  
   Required exact content (5 lines, LF-terminated):  
     4 artifact-beta-2.1.0.jar  
     3 artifact-alpha-1.0.0.jar  
     3 artifact-gamma-3.3.7.jar  
     2 artifact-delta-4.0.0.jar  
     1 artifact-epsilon-5.2.1.jar  

No additional files should be created inside /home/user/project/reports/.
